title: Recursion - a function that calls itself
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A recursive function solves a problem by calling itself on a smaller piece, until it hits a base case that stops the chain.
explanation: Recursion needs two things: a base case (the smallest version, answered directly, no more calls) and a recursive case (do a little, then call yourself on something smaller). factorial(n) is n times factorial(n-1), and it stops at factorial(1) = 1. Miss the base case and it never stops - the calls stack up forever. Get it right and big problems fold neatly into small ones.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: factorial(4) is 4 * factorial(3), and so on down.

exercises.question: What stops a recursion from calling itself forever?
hints: It's the smallest version, answered directly.
